name: SurrealDB
description: Multi-model database combining document, graph, relational, time-series, and vector data models with real-time capabilities and edge deployment options.

license.comment: Business Source License 1.1 with automatic conversion to Apache-2.0 after 4 years. SDKs and libraries are Apache-2.0 or MIT.

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted with various storage backends (RocksDB, TiKV, SurrealKV). Surreal Cloud launched December 2024 as official managed service.

initial-release.comment: First version completed in 2017 (Go), source made public in 2021, v1.0 released September 2023. Rust rewrite is the current version.

data-model.comment: Multi-model database supporting document, graph, relational, time-series, geospatial and key-value data types with vector search capabilities.

topology.comment: In distributed mode, uses TiKV as storage backend which implements Raft consensus with leader-follower topology. SurrealDB compute nodes are stateless.

sharding.comment: When using TiKV backend, data is automatically sharded by range with no manual sharding required. TiKV handles data partitioning and replication.

replication-factor.comment: In distributed mode with TiKV, inherits TiKV's default of 3 replicas. Single-node mode has no replication.

multi-region.comment: Supported via TiKV backend which enables geo-distributed deployments. Surreal Cloud supports multi-region configurations.

cap-position.comment: When using TiKV backend, inherits TiKV's CP (consistency-focused) behavior with Raft consensus.

consistency-model.comment: Supports read-your-own-writes consistency with read snapshots for consistent database views. MVCC support with snapshot isolation.

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID compliance with support for multi-row, multi-table transactions with no duration limit.

transaction-scope.comment: Supports transactions across multiple rows and tables. In distributed mode with TiKV, provides global cross-partition transactions.

isolation-levels.comment: Snapshot isolation via MVCC. Supports non-blocking concurrent reads and writes.

distributed-transactions.comment: When using TiKV backend, leverages TiKV's distributed transaction support. Designed to handle multi-table transactions without row/table locks.

query-language.comment: Uses SurrealQL, a SQL-like query language with extensions for graph traversal, relations, and multi-model operations.

sql-compatibility.comment: SurrealQL is SQL-inspired but not SQL-compatible. Has unique syntax for graph edges, record links, and multi-model features.

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports traditional indexes, unique indexes/constraints, full-text search indexes, and vector embedding indexes.

joins.comment: Supports relations via RELATE statements creating graph edges. Graph traversal syntax enables relationship navigation.

aggregations.comment: Built-in aggregate analytics views with support for moving averages, time-based windowing, and attribute-based counting.

full-text-search.comment: Built-in full-text search with custom analyzers, multiple tokenizers, BM25/TF-IDF ranking, and support for 17 languages.

max-cluster-size.comment: Claims scalability to 'hundreds of nodes' via TiKV backend, but no specific tested cluster size documented.

write-throughput.comment: No published benchmarks available. Performance depends on storage backend (RocksDB, TiKV, SurrealKV).

auto-scaling.comment: SurrealDB compute nodes are stateless and can be scaled independently. Storage scaling depends on backend (TiKV provides automatic scaling).

hot-spot-handling.comment: Via TiKV backend which handles automatic Region splitting and rebalancing. SurrealKV is still under optimization.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Stateless compute nodes enable rolling upgrades. Kubernetes deployments support rolling updates via Helm charts.

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema-flexible with support for schemaless and schemafull modes. Schema changes don't require downtime.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: SurrealKV supports versioned queries for historical data access, but full PITR requires external backup tooling.

backup-strategy.comment: SQL export/import for backup and restoration. Surreal Cloud provides managed backups.

kubernetes-operator.comment: No official Kubernetes operator. Provides Helm charts and uses TiDB Operator for TiKV backend deployments.

rust-driver.comment: Official surrealdb.rs SDK. Supports SurrealDB v2.0.0 to v2.4.0. Requires Rust 1.80.1+.

dotnet-driver.comment: Official SurrealDb.Net SDK. Supports .NET 6.0+ and .NET Standard 2.1+. Available on NuGet.

ruby-driver.comment: Community 'mylk' library provides Ruby client with HTTP and WebSocket support. Not an official SDK.

c-cpp-driver.comment: Official C driver (surrealdb.c) supports WebSocket connections. No dedicated C++ driver.

orm-support.comment: No mainstream ORM support. Prisma support requested but not implemented. Community surreal_orm for Rust exists.

open-source.comment: BSL-1.1 is source-available but not OSI-approved open source. Converts to Apache-2.0 after 4 years.

free-tier.comment: Surreal Cloud offers a free tier for prototypes and getting started.

pricing-model.comment: Surreal Cloud starts at $0.02/hour with compute and storage pricing. Enterprise tier available for on-premise or private cloud.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Starting at $0.02/hour ≈ $15/month for basic tier. Actual costs depend on compute and storage requirements.

change-data-capture.comment: Built-in Change Feeds for streaming data changes. Supports SINCE <time> syntax for historical changes.

watch-subscribe.comment: Live Queries allow real-time subscriptions to data changes with row-level authorization. Supports DIFF-PATCH-MATCH for efficient updates.

triggers.comment: Supports event triggers that execute on data changes.

event-streaming.comment: Can integrate with Kafka, MQTT, or Airflow in event processing pipelines. Live queries provide custom real-time streaming.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Stack Overflow tag exists but specific question count not available. Community primarily uses Discord and GitHub.
